digest.greeting: Jean, today closed a few concrete follow-ups: the Venn ticket escalation, the TRACE UAT follow-up, the Phase 2 SMS handoff, and one PMO/CM-CoE intake routing. The remaining focus is to keep Staff Augmentation, the Send Letters timeline, and Friday's EPMO material leadership-ready.

items.label: Closed the Venn ticket escalation carry-over
items.detail: The consolidated Venn ticket-number follow-up is now marked complete after carrying forward from the prior EOD as the main unresolved escalation path.

items.label: Closed the TRACE tasksets UAT follow-up
items.detail: The Yuri follow-up on TRACE tasksets ready for UAT is now marked complete after being carried into today's reminder list.

items.label: Confirmed the Phase 2 SMS handoff path for IT
items.detail: The Phase 2 SMS handoff item is now marked complete after today's checklist focused on approvals, task lists, and named UAT testers before Friday's IT sync.

items.label: Routed the AOS-Rejection Taskset intake
items.detail: Jean sent the PMO and CM-CoE intake notifications and the requester acknowledgement for Javier Hurtado's AOS-Rejection Taskset request, with Mar Garcia and David Molano copied on the requester response.

items.label: Secure Staff Augmentation IT ownership and scope
items.detail: Keep the next step focused on a clear IT lead, Rippling/Microsoft 365 integration alignment, and permission for Salt Click to begin solution architecture. Nadia's meeting packet framed the current manual tracker and rising court volume as a business-critical risk.

items.label: Bring back a realistic Send Letters Queue timeline
items.detail: The morning digest tied this to Anu's stalled-project thread and Jhara's commitment to return after Procurement with Baesman status, parallel workstreams, next steps, and a target go-live path.

items.label: Shape Friday's EPMO presentation storyline
items.detail: Use Friday's protected prep windows and leadership touchpoints to decide the proof points and whether Staff Augmentation, CoE metrics, Detention Prevention, and executive-project status should be framed as wins, risks, or follow-ups.

items.label: Review the new Billing intake from Shannon Cooper
items.detail: A new unread intake alert arrived late today from Janet McDowell for a Billing submission by Shannon Cooper. Review the Asana-linked request and decide the right routing or next action.

items.label: TRACE & Staff Aug touchpoint
items.detail: Use this as the first Friday checkpoint for today's TRACE UAT completion and any Staff Augmentation ownership follow-through.

items.label: Phase 2 Weekly IT Projects Sync
items.detail: Bring the Phase 2 SMS handoff status, UAT tester names, and any TRACE readiness notes that need IT visibility.

items.label: CoE Sync Jean
items.detail: A good window to align with Mar and David on CoE ownership, intake routing, documentation cleanup, and what should carry into EPMO reporting.

items.label: Blocked Space
items.detail: Protect for EPMO EOW prep, executive-project slides, and any Staff Augmentation or Send Letters follow-up from today.

items.label: EPMO EOW Check In and PM Team session overlap
items.detail: Outlook shows EPMO EOW Check In and Get to Know the Project Management Team starting at the same time. Sara moved the EPMO check-in earlier and added Nadia for programmatic updates.

items.label: Executive Project Status Slide Update
items.detail: Weekly deck update with Yuri, Jhara, and Mar; this is the natural deadline for the Friday EPMO presentation storyline.

items.label: IT Litify Sprint #12 Review
items.detail: Sprint review with IT Software, project managers, and stakeholders; useful if SMS, UAT, or Litify-related dependencies need visibility.
